news_items.title: Court of Appeal refuses additional written round
news_items.title_zh: 上诉庭拒绝额外书面轮次：一审辅助请求自动纳入上诉
news_items.summary: Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care – Court of Appeal, 27 February 2026 (UPC_CoA_884/2025)
news_items.content: Court of Appeal refuses additional written round – auxiliary requests from first instance already part of the appeal Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care – Court of Appeal, 27 February 2026 (UPC_CoA_884/2025) Introduction In an recent order, the Court of Appeal rejected Sibio’s request for an additional exchange of written pleadings in appeal proceedings concerning EP 3 831 283. Sibio had argued that a further written round was necessary because Abbott relied in its response on six auxiliary requests that had not been addressed by the Court of First Instance. The judge-rapporteur dismissed the request and closed the written phase. Auxiliary requests from first instance remain part of the appeal Abbott had filed the auxiliary requests already during the proceedings before the Central Division, where they had been admitted into the case. The first-instance decision ultimately upheld the patent as granted and therefore did not need to address those requests. On appeal, Sibio argued that it should be allowed further written submissions because Abbott had relied again on these auxiliary requests in its response. The Court of Appeal rejected this argument. Referring to Rules 222.1 and 222.2 RoP, it emphasised that the appeal proceedings are based on the requests, facts and evidence submitted at first instance. Since the auxiliary requests had already been filed and admitted there, they automatically form part of the appeal proceedings unless the respondent withdraws them. The Court also clarified that there is no requirement to re-file auxiliary requests on appeal . No cross-appeal and no right to an additional written round The Court further rejected Sibio’s suggestion that Abbott’s reliance on the auxiliary requests amounted to a conditional cross-appeal. Abbott did not challenge the outcome of the first-instance decision and merely maintained the requests it had already made in the earlier proceedings. For the same reason, there was no basis for allowing additional written pleadings under Rule 36 RoP. The Court stressed that the mere fact that additional pleadings would not affect the procedural timetable is not a sufficient justification. Nor did the refusal violate the right to be heard. Sibio had already had the opportunity to address the auxiliary requests during the first-instance proceedings and in its statement of grounds of appeal, and it could respond further during the oral hearing. Takeaways Auxiliary requests filed and admitted at first instance automatically form part of the appeal proceedings. A respondent relying on such requests does not bring a cross-appeal if it seeks only to maintain the outcome of the first-instance decision. Rule 36 RoP does not entitle parties to an additional written round merely because the opposing party relies on auxiliary requests already present in the case. Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care – Court of Appeal, 27 February 2026 (UPC_CoA_884/2025) In an recent order, the Court of Appeal rejected Sibio’s request for an additional exchange of written pleadings in appeal proceedings concerning EP 3 831 283. Sibio had argued that a further written round was necessary because Abbott relied in its response on six auxiliary requests that had not been addressed by the Court of First Instance. The judge-rapporteur dismissed the request and closed the written phase. Abbott had filed the auxiliary requests already during the proceedings before the Central Division, where they had been admitted into the case. The first-instance decision ultimately upheld the patent as granted and therefore did not need to address those requests. On appeal, Sibio argued that it should be allowed further written submissions because Abbott had relied again on these auxiliary requests in its response. The Court of Appeal rejected this argument. Referring to Rules 222.1 and 222.2 RoP, it emphasised that the appeal proceedings are based on the requests, facts and evidence submitted at first instance. Since the auxiliary requests had already been filed and admitted there, they automatically form part of the appeal proceedings unless the respondent withdraws them. The Court also clarified that there is no requirement to re-file auxiliary requests on appeal . The Court further rejected Sibio’s suggestion that Abbott’s reliance on the auxiliary requests amounted to a conditional cross-appeal. Abbott did not challenge the outcome of the first-instance decision and merely maintained the requests it had already made in the earlier proceedings. For the same reason, there was no basis for allowing additional written pleadings under Rule 36 RoP. The Court stressed that the mere fact that additional pleadings would not affect the procedural timetable is not a sufficient justification.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在 Sibio 诉 Abbott 案中裁定，驳回上诉人要求增加书面答辩轮次的请求。法院明确，一审已受理的辅助请求自动构成上诉案件一部分，无需重新提交；被上诉人仅维持原判结果不构成交叉上诉，故无权触发额外书面程序。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中应重视一审阶段的请求布局，所有一审辅助请求将自动带入上诉。若作为上诉人，需预判对方可能援引的旧请求并提前准备；作为被上诉人，可利用此规则避免程序拖延，稳固胜诉成果。

news_items.title: Intellectual Property Office fees to increase from April 2026
news_items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局将于 2026 年 4 月起上调知识产权官费
news_items.summary: The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) is increasing fees for patents, trade marks and designs from 1 April 2026.
news_items.content: The Intellectual Property Office (IPO) is increasing fees for patents, trade marks and designs from 1 April 2026.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）正式发布通知，确认自 2026 年 4 月 1 日起，将全面上调专利、商标及外观设计的官方申请与维护费用。此次调整涵盖该机构管辖下的主要知识产权类型，旨在反映运营成本变化。对于计划在英国进行知识产权布局的权利人而言，需提前规划预算，以应对即将到来的成本上升，确保存量与增量业务的连续性。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在英布局的中国企业应立即核查现有专利与商标的续费节点，若恰逢 2026 年 4 月后到期，需预留额外预算。建议利用窗口期加速提交 pending 申请，锁定当前较低费率，同时重新评估英国市场 IP 组合的投入产出比。

news_items.summary: Nowadays, dental prostheses are no longer elaborately modelled by dental technicians, but milled in one piece from special blanks. These usually consist of several layers of colour, for example to realistically highlight the gums and teeth. The dispute between Ivoclar Vivadent and its Chinese competitor Huliang Bio-Tech revolves around such flesh- and tooth-coloured blanks. These usually consist of several layers of colour, for example to realistically highlight the gums and teeth. news_items.ai_summary_zh: 牙科修复体正从手工模型转向多层色毛坯铣削制造。列支敦士登巨头 Ivoclar Vivadent 与中国竞争者 Huliang Bio-Tech 就此类肉色与牙色毛坯发生专利纠纷。在 Graf von Westphalen 律所代理下，中国制造商成功抵御 Ivoclar 的指控并 prevailing，凸显了欧洲专利诉讼中技术细节与法律策略的关键作用。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出海需重视欧洲专利布局与侵权风险应对。本案表明，借助当地顶级律所专业力量，可有效抵御跨国巨头诉讼。建议企业加强核心技术自由实施分析，建立常态化海外维权机制。

news_items.title: Longevity Law in Germany: Intellectual Property and Regulatory Pathways for the Emerging Healthspan Industry | Grünecker
news_items.title_zh: 德国长寿法：健康寿命产业的知识产权与监管路径
news_items.summary: Longevity law in Germany: how IP protection, EU regulation and data governance shape the development and commercialization of healthspan technologies.
news_items.content:
by Dr. Julia Traumann | 09. March 2026 | News

Scientific advances in biotechnology, data science, regenerative medicine and artificial intelligence are accelerating the development of therapies and technologies aimed at extending human healthspan, the period of life spent in good health. This rapidly emerging sector, often referred to as the longevity or healthspan industry, is attracting significant investment and producing novel approaches to addressing the biological mechanisms of aging.

For companies operating in this area, legal strategy is becoming as important as scientific progress. Longevity technologies must navigate a complex intersection of intellectual property protection, pharmaceutical regulation, medical device law and data governance frameworks. In Europe and Germany, patent strategy and regulatory classification will be critical in determining whether longevity innovations can successfully reach the market.

This article outlines some of the key considerations shaping the development and commercialization of longevity technologies in Germany and across the European Union.

The longevity industry has grown rapidly in recent years as scientific research increasingly focuses on the biological processes underlying aging. Rather than treating individual diseases in isolation, longevity research seeks to address fundamental cellular and molecular mechanisms as well as general principles for life (diet, exercise, family/friends/social interaction) that contribute to age-related decline.

Major biotechnology ventures have emerged around this scientific paradigm. These companies operate at the intersection of biotechnology, data science, and advanced therapeutics, often combining drug development, biomarker discovery, and AI-driven research platforms.

The rapid growth of this sector has begun to generate a distinct set of legal questions that extend beyond traditional fields of law. These questions form the basis of what is increasingly described as longevity law, an interdisciplinary legal framework addressing intellectual property, regulatory strategy, and commercialization in the healthspan economy.

One of the defining characteristics of longevity innovation is that it rarely fits neatly within a single category. Instead, longevity technologies frequently combine elements of multiple sectors, including:

artificial intelligence and digital health platforms;

nutraceutical and wellness products.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析德国及欧盟长寿产业面临的法律挑战，指出该领域融合生物技术、AI 与数字健康，需跨越专利保护、药品法规及数据治理等多重框架。文章强调，正确的专利策略与监管分类是创新技术成功商业化的关键，并定义了涵盖 IP 与合规的跨学科“长寿法”概念。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局长寿科技时，应提前规划专利与监管双重策略。鉴于技术跨界特性，需精准界定产品属性以匹配合规路径，避免因分类错误导致市场准入受阻或 IP 保护落空。

news_items.title: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
news_items.title_zh: EUIPO 于雅典展示 IPEP：以数字工具强化知识产权保护
news_items.summary: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
news_items.content: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools The EUIPO´s IP Enforcement Portal (IPEP) was presented during the 1st edition of the ‘training for officers on the field’ co‑organised by the Greek Independent Authority for Market Control and Consumer’s Protection and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group (GACG), represented by INDICAM. The event was supported by the EUIPO, and took place last week, 27 February 2026 in Athens, Greece. It gathered representatives of all Greek competent authorities that detect and seize counterfeit such as customs, municipal police and the authority for market control. The training addressed officers working on the field, being on the front line to protect the Greek market from counterfeit items. It brought together right holders and national authorities to exchange practical information on the detection of fakes. About the event The one-day hands-on training was co‑organised by the Greek Independent Authority for Market Control and Consumer’s Protection and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group, represented by INDICAM, with support from the EUIPO. The training aimed to support frontline law enforcement officers in targeting and detecting counterfeit goods. It provided practical information on frequently counterfeited products such as automobile spare parts, fashion apparel, and tobacco products presented by the right holders themselves. Around 15 companies presented their IP portfolios, key infringement challenges, and technical guidance for identifying counterfeit items. IPEP: A unified digital tool for enforcement authorities At the event, the EUIPO team showcased how IPEP supports Greek and EU enforcement officers by enabling secure, real‑time information exchange between authorities and rights holders. IPEP Mobile App The team also presented the mobile version of IPEP, offering field‑ready access to: Multilingual, real‑time search to IP rights, product and contact point information on the spot. suspicious‑case (detention notification) creation and syncing— all optimised for rapid use by Greek enforcement officers. Moving Forward The seminar reaffirmed the commitment of the Greek national authorities, industry stakeholders represented by the GACG and the EUIPO to deepen cooperation and expand the use of digital enforcement tools like IPEP across Greece. Additional training sessions and onboarding support will continue throughout 2026–2027.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）在希腊雅典联合当地市场监管机构及全球反假冒组织，举办针对一线执法人员的培训活动。会议重点展示了知识产权执法门户（IPEP）及其移动应用，旨在通过数字化手段实现权利人与执法部门间的实时信息交换，提升对汽车零件、时尚及烟草等假冒商品的查获效率，并计划于 2026 至 2027 年深化在希腊的合作与推广。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业应关注欧盟数字化执法趋势，主动利用 IPEP 平台备案知识产权。建议企业配合当地执法部门提供真伪鉴别技术支持，建立快速响应机制，以降低产品在希腊及欧盟市场的被侵权风险。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 中国前五大智能手机制造商荣耀宣布加入 Via Licensing 管理的 AAC 音频编码专利池。此举标志着荣耀选择通过无诉讼的商业许可路径解决标准必要专利问题，避免了潜在的法律纠纷。该交易反映了全球 SEP 许可市场中，主要终端厂商倾向于通过专利池快速获得合规授权，以保障产品在全球包括欧洲市场的自由运营。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 建议中国出海企业评估核心标准技术专利状态，优先通过专利池获取授权以规避欧洲高额诉讼风险。同时应建立 FRAND 谈判预案，平衡许可成本与合规效率，确保在欧洲市场的持续稳定经营。

news_items.title: Amazon-InterDigital dispute and landmark CJEU referral put UPC jurisdiction in focus
news_items.title_zh: 亚马逊与 InterDigital 纠纷及 CJEU 转呈聚焦 UPC 管辖权
news_items.summary: A recent UK court ruling calls for clearer cross-court communication while the UPC asks Europe’s top court for clarity on the use of long-arm jurisdiction
news_items.content: A recent UK court ruling calls for clearer cross-court communication while the UPC asks Europe’s top court for clarity on the use of long-arm jurisdiction
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国法院近期裁定呼吁加强跨国司法沟通，与此同时，统一专利法院（UPC）就长臂管辖权的适用问题向欧盟法院（CJEU）寻求澄清。该动态源于亚马逊与 InterDigital 的标准必要专利争端，标志着 UPC 管辖范围面临关键法律测试，可能重塑欧洲 SEP 诉讼格局。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉足 SEP 许可或诉讼时，需高度关注 UPC 管辖权边界变化。建议提前评估长臂管辖风险，优化全球诉讼策略，并利用英欧司法互动窗口期加强合规布局。

news_items.title: EPO meets with Novartis
news_items.title_zh: EPO 与诺华举行会议探讨专利审查时效与统一专利体系
news_items.summary: On 4 February, the European Patent Office (EPO) held an online meeting with the Swiss pharmaceutical and biotechnology company Novartis.
news_items.content: On 4 February, the European Patent Office (EPO) held an online meeting with the Swiss pharmaceutical and biotechnology company Novartis. The exchanges, which form part of the EPO’s structured engagement with large applicants, marked the fourth bilateral meeting of this kind in 2026 and the 110 th since the EPO first launched its initiative to deepen user dialogue in 2021. The Novartis delegation was led by Head of Commercial IP Marco Riensche and included more than 20 representatives based at the company’s headquarters in Basel, as well as in the United States and the United Kingdom. The EPO delegation was led by Vice-President Patent Granting Process Steve Rowan, supported by managers and experts working in the fields of pharmaceuticals, biotechnology and patent law. Filing trends and investment considerations Novartis representatives emphasised timeliness of patent examination as being critical for companies’ investment decisions, especially with regard to new formulations and dosage regimens, as well as further medical uses. As Mr. Riensche emphasised, " a timely decision on whether a patent will be granted or upheld is crucial for planning and investment decisions" . Unitary Patent and supplementary protection certificates Novartis maintains a cautious but positive approach to the Unitary Patent, and representatives expressed strong support for a centralised supplementary protection certificate system with invalidity requests and appeals handled in the Unified Patent Court. They highlighted the benefits of EU-wide consistency, as well as streamlined procedures and agreed that the EPO is a suitable authority to implement such a system. Feedback on the latest Guidelines Recent revisions to the Guidelines for Examination regarding antibody-related patent applications were also discussed. The EPO highlighted the public online consultation that commences in the first quarter of every year, offering all users the opportunity to submit feedback on the preview versions of each set of Guidelines, ahead of their entry into force on 1 April. Dialogue continues Mr Riensche thanked the EPO for the constructive meeting and welcomed continued dialogue, noting that “ there is still much more to discuss .” About Novartis Novartis is a global pharmaceutical company with a strong research focus. In the Patent Index for 2024, Novartis ranked 13 th among Swiss applicants at the EPO, with 120 filings. In 2025, Novartis invested more than USD 10 billion in research and development and employed more than 20 000 R&D staff worldwide, with a significant footprint in Europe (more than 9 000 R&D staff). The company has the third-largest drug pipeline among pharmaceutical companies globally and maintains an in-house IP team of around 80 patent attorneys worldwide. Further information
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 2 月 4 日，欧洲专利局（EPO）与瑞士诺华公司举行线上会议，重点讨论专利审查时效对投资决策的关键影响。双方就统一专利制度及建立集中化补充保护证书（SPC）体系交换意见，诺华支持由 EPO 实施该体系并交由统一专利法院处理无效诉讼。此外，会议还涉及抗体相关专利申请审查指南的修订反馈机制。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应关注欧洲专利审查提速趋势，优化布局策略。同时需重视统一专利法院在 SPC 纠纷中的核心地位，提前构建应对集中化无效挑战的预案，利用指南修订窗口期提升申请质量。

news_items.title: The first CJEU referral from the UPC
news_items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院就长臂管辖问题首次向 CJEU 提请初步裁决
news_items.summary: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by ...
news_items.content: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by the UPC Court of Appeal on 6 March in Dyson v Dreame & Eurep case, in which it stayed the appeal proceedings insofar as they related to the territory of Spain and Eurep (the Authorised Representative for manufacturers based in a non-EU Member State); and requested that the CJEU give a preliminary ruling on 4 questions relating to long arm jurisdiction and anchor defendants.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 在戴森诉追觅及 Eurep 案中，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院于 3 月 6 日发布命令，暂停涉及西班牙领土及非欧盟制造商授权代表的上诉程序。法院正式请求欧盟法院（CJEU）就长臂管辖权及锚定被告问题作出四项初步裁决。此举标志着 UPC 首次启动向 CJEU 的转介程序，旨在厘清跨境专利侵权诉讼中的关键管辖权界限。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需高度关注此案，因其直接决定非欧盟企业在欧被诉风险。若 CJEU 确认宽泛管辖权，中国企业即便无欧盟实体也可能面临诉讼。建议提前评估欧洲业务架构，制定应对长臂管辖的合规与诉讼策略。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院在戴森案中首次暂停部分程序并转介 CJEU", "核心争议聚焦于针对非欧盟实体的长臂管辖权适用范围", "CJEU 的初步裁决将确立 UPC 体系下锚定被告的法律标准"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非欧盟制造企业面临被直接纳入 UPC 长臂管辖的潜在风险", "亟需根据裁决预期调整欧洲市场准入架构与抗辩预案"]

news_items.title: Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks
news_items.title_zh: Thom Browne 诉阿迪达斯案：非传统商标面临的挑战
news_items.summary: Kyrana Hulstein and Haydn Walker, ‘Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks (March 2026) Int...
news_items.content: Kyrana Hulstein and Haydn Walker, ‘ Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland : The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks (March 2026) Intellectual Property Forum Journal 143.' In two recent United Kingdom (“UK”) Court of Appeal decisions, the courts considered the meaning of a “sign” in section 1 of the Trade Marks Act 1994 (the “TMA”). In particular, the courts navigated the interplay between pictorial representations and written descriptions and the difficulties associated with colour and position marks. Section 1(1) of the TMA defines a trade mark as a sign capable of being represented in the register and distinguishing the goods or services of one undertaking from those of other undertakings. A trade mark may consist of words, designs, letters, numerals, colours, sounds or the shape of goods or their packaging. In 2019, section 1(1) was amended to include the wording “in a manner which enables the registrar and other competent authorities and the public to determine the clear and precise subject matter of the protection afforded to the proprietor”. This amendment codified the requirements for the registrability of a trade mark set out in Sieckmann , 1 namely that a trade mark must be clear, objective, precise, self-contained, durable, intelligible and easily accessible (known as the Sieckmann criteria). In two decisions handed down on the same date, Arnold LJ, sitting in the Court of Appeal, took the opportunity to clarify how the Sieckmann criteria apply in practice: first, in Thom Browne v adidas 2 and then, in Babek v Iceland. 3 Each of these decisions is discussed in turn below. These proceedings arose from long-running tensions between well-known sportswear brand adidas and luxury fashion brand Thom Browne over the use of stripes. Thom Browne sought declarations that sixteen of adidas’ UK trade mark registrations were invalid due to lack of registrability and distinctive character (sections 1(1) and 3(1)(b) of the TMA, respectively) or, alternatively, should be revoked for non-use. The registrations in issue covered three stripes applied to articles of clothing, footwear, bags and hats.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在 Thom Browne 诉阿迪达斯等案中，重新审视了《1994 年商标法》关于“标志”的定义。法院重点厘清了图形表示与文字描述在非传统商标（如颜色、位置标记）中的相互作用，并明确了 Sieckmann 标准（清晰、精确、持久等）在实践中的具体适用，裁定涉案三条纹商标需满足更严格的注册可确定性要求。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请颜色或位置等非传统商标时，须确保图示与文字描述高度一致且精确，避免因定义模糊被驳回。面对大牌诉讼，应善用 Sieckmann 标准挑战对方商标有效性，同时提升自身商标布局的严谨性以规避侵权风险。

news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非传统商标申请需严格符合清晰精确标准以防无效", "利用判例标准可有效应对国际大牌的商标维权攻势"]

news_items.title: KPN takes Oppo dispute to India, expands UPC campaign
news_items.title_zh: KPN 将 Oppo 纠纷扩至印度并强化 UPC 诉讼
news_items.summary: A WebRTC case – the first in India – is the latest action in a protracted spat between the Dutch telecoms company and India’s second-largest smartphone vendor
news_items.content: A WebRTC case – the first in India – is the latest action in a protracted spat between the Dutch telecoms company and India’s second-largest smartphone vendor
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 荷兰电信巨头 KPN 与智能手机厂商 Oppo 的长期专利纠纷升级。继在欧洲统一专利法院（UPC）提起诉讼后，KPN 首次在印度针对 WebRTC 技术发起诉讼。此举标志着其全球执法策略的扩张，旨在通过多法域联动施压，解决标准必要专利许可争议。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需警惕权利人多法域围剿策略，尤其在 UPC 生效背景下。建议提前评估 SEP 许可风险，建立全球诉讼应对机制，避免单一市场失利引发连锁反应，同时关注印度等新兴市场的司法动态。

news_items.title: International Patent Applications Rose in 2025, with Digital Communication and Semiconductor Technologies Showing Strong Growth
news_items.title_zh: 2025 年国际专利申请微增，数字通信与半导体增长强劲
news_items.summary: International patent applications filed through WIPO's Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 filings worldwide, marking a second consecutive year of growth. The increase was partly due to robust activity in ICT related fields, with digital communication remaining as the top category (11.1%) of published PCT applications, while semiconductors recorded one of the fastest growth rates among all leading areas of international patents.
news_items.content: International patent applications filed through WIPO's Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 filings worldwide, marking a second consecutive year of growth. The increase was partly due to robust activity in ICT related fields, with digital communication remaining as the top category (11.1%) of published PCT applications, while semiconductors recorded one of the fastest growth rates among all leading areas of international patents.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织发布数据显示，2025 年通过 PCT 提交的国际专利申请量同比增长 0.7%，达 27.59 万件，连续第二年实现增长。增长主要由 ICT 领域驱动，其中数字通信稳居第一大技术类别，占比 11.1%；半导体技术则成为增速最快的领先领域之一，凸显全球在关键硬件技术上的竞争加剧。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局应紧跟半导体与数字通信热点，利用 PCT 途径优化海外申请策略。鉴于欧洲市场对硬科技的高度重视，建议加大高价值专利储备，防范潜在的技术壁垒与侵权风险，提升在全球产业链中的话语权。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年 PCT 国际专利申请总量微增 0.7%，连续两年保持上升态势", "数字通信以 11.1% 占比持续领跑，半导体领域录得最快增长率之一", "ICT 相关领域的 robust 活动是推动全球专利申请增长的主要动力"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["聚焦半导体与数字通信赛道，利用 PCT 途径加速欧洲市场专利布局", "针对欧洲硬科技竞争态势，提升高价值专利储备以应对潜在壁垒"]

news_items.title: Celebrating International Women’s Day
news_items.title_zh: EPO 庆祝国际妇女节并发布女性 STEM 领域研究报告
news_items.summary: Action for equality: joint statement and the latest study
news_items.content: On 8 March, the European Patent Office (EPO) will join organisations around the world in marking International Women’s Day (IWD) – a moment to celebrate the achievements of women, recognise the progress made, and reaffirm our shared commitment to diversity and inclusion (D&I) in the workplace. As a mark of solidarity, the EPO logo will be changed to purple, the official colour of International Women’s Day. Partnering with other IP offices to defend women’s and girls’ rights The EPO has adopted the 2026 UN theme “Rights, Justice, Action: For ALL Women and Girls”, partnering with WIPO and 82 national offices to sign a joint statement highlighting women’s underrepresentation within the IP system. The signatories emphasise that “ all women and girls have equal rights to protect their inventions, creations and innovations through accessible IP systems. They have the right to access and navigate IP processes without prohibitive barriers, to own and control the commercial outcomes of their intellectual contributions, and to be counted, visible and recognised as innovators and creators in all fields .” EPO study ‘Advancing women in STEM’ published this week This week, the EPO Observatory published a study revealing slow growth in the share of women in science, technology, mathematics and engineering (STEM). Despite incremental gains, women remain underrepresented as inventors, founders and patent professionals. The study highlights structural barriers and offers evidence to advance policy and equality strategies to foster inclusive innovation. Designed to inspire and inform practical action, the study also profiles four accomplished women inventors: Dame Carol Robinson and Cordelia Schmid, recipients of the European Inventor Award, and Pilar Granado and Marie Perrin, winners of the Young Inventors Prize. About International Women's Day Rooted in the women’s suffrage movement, International Women’s Day has been observed worldwide for more than a hundred years. Europe’s first celebrations took place in 1911, quickly gaining support in Austria, Denmark, Germany and Switzerland. In 1975, the United Nations began officially observing the day, and in 1977 it encouraged member states to recognise it as a UN-designated holiday. news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）为庆祝 2026 年国际妇女节，联合 WIPO 等 82 个机构签署声明，呼吁消除女性在知识产权系统中的代表性不足问题。同期，EPO 观察站发布研究报告，揭示 STEM 领域女性占比增长缓慢，指出结构性障碍，并通过四位杰出女性发明人案例，为推动包容性创新政策提供实证依据。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧洲 IP 体系对多样性的重视，优化内部创新激励机制以吸引女性人才。同时，利用 EPO 提供的教育服务，提升女性研发人员的专利布局能力，契合欧盟政策导向，降低合规与声誉风险。

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）宣布恢复月度统计数据发布，并启用新格式以提供更清晰的洞察。截至 2026 年 2 月 28 日，一审法院共受理案件 1130 起，其中 2026 年前两个月新增 72 起。数据显示德国杜塞尔多夫和慕尼黑分院案件量领先，英语为最主要诉讼语言。新数据涵盖侵权、无效及临时措施等细分类型，全面展示法院活动趋势。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注 UPC 诉讼高地（如杜塞尔多夫）的动态，提前布局应对策略。鉴于英语案件占比高，建议提升涉外诉讼语言能力，并利用公开数据分析竞争对手在欧专利布局与诉讼倾向，优化风险防控。

news_items.title: Upcoming changes to judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris
news_items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉院及巴黎一审分部法官组成变更公告
news_items.summary: The Court informs on changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective as of 1 April 2026
news_items.content: The Court informs on changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective as of 1 April 2026 Following the retirement of Ms Emanuela Germano, legally qualified judge at the Court of Appeal, at the end of March 2026, the Administrative Committee, by decision D-AC/03/02072025 of 2 July 2025, nominated Mr Paolo Catallozzi, legally qualified judge at the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, to be appointed as legally qualified judge of the Court of Appeal as of 1 April 2026. Following the promotion of Mr Paolo Catallozzi to the Court of Appeal, the Administrative Committee also decided on 2 July 2025 (D-AC/04/02072025) to appoint Mr Vincenzo Carn ì as legally qualified judge as of 1 April 2026. The President of the Court of Appeal, Mr Klaus Grabinski and the President of the Court of First Instance, Ms Florence Butin, thank Ms Germano for her valuable work for the Court of Appeal and wish her an enjoyable retirement. The Presidents wish Mr Catallozzi a successful start in his new position at the Court of Appeal and Mr Carnì a warm welcome to the Court of First Instance.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院宣布自 2026 年 4 月 1 日起调整司法人员组成。因上诉院法官 Germano 退休，原巴黎中央分部一审法官 Catallozzi 晋升至上诉院；同时任命 Carnì为新任巴黎一审法官。该决定由行政委员会于 2025 年 7 月作出，旨在确保法院审判工作的连续性与稳定性。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 法官更替可能影响特定技术领域的审判风格与倾向性。在欧中企需关注新任法官背景，评估对未决案件或未来诉讼策略的潜在影响，适时调整法律团队配置以应对巴黎分部的司法实践变化。

news_items.title: [Guest Post] Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape
news_items.title_zh: 博洛尼亚法院解析后 Cofemel 时代的设计与版权保护
news_items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest post by Katfriend Luigi Manna (attorney at Martini Manna & Partners), commenting on a decision from the Bologna IP Court. Here is what Luigi writes:  Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape by Luigi Manna A recent ruling of the IP Court
news_items.content: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest post by Katfriend Luigi Manna (attorney at Martini Manna & Partners), commenting on a decision from the Bologna IP Court. Here is what Luigi writes:  Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape by Luigi Manna A recent ruling of the IP Court
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析意大利博洛尼亚知识产权法院最新判决，探讨在后 Cofemel 判例背景下，蛇形产品的外观设计、立体商标与版权保护的界限。律师 Luigi Manna 指出，法院严格审视了艺术价值标准，厘清了不同权利类型对同一形状的叠加保护逻辑，为欧盟成员国处理类似案件提供了重要参考。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口造型独特产品时，需警惕多重 IP 风险。建议提前评估形状的艺术高度，避免盲目主张版权；同时应针对意大利等关键市场制定差异化的外观与商标布局策略，以防侵权诉讼。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["博洛尼亚法院裁定蛇形形状的艺术价值认定标准", "厘清外观设计、立体商标与版权的重叠保护边界", "后 Cofemel 时代欧盟各国司法实践的统一性挑战"]

news_items.title: NovaCloud strikes Oracle licence as Google, Amazon and Meta defend suits
news_items.title_zh: NovaCloud 获甲骨文许可并起诉多家云巨头
news_items.summary: Powered by patents from Ericsson, NovaCloud reached a private licence deal with Oracle, but has sued six other cloud computing leaders
news_items.content: Powered by patents from Ericsson, NovaCloud reached a private licence deal with Oracle, but has sued six other cloud computing leaders
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 依托爱立信专利组合，实体 NovaCloud 已与甲骨文达成私下许可协议。与此同时，该公司正对包括谷歌、亚马逊和 Meta 在内的六家云计算领导者发起专利侵权诉讼。此案反映了标准必要专利持有者通过专门实体在欧洲数字基础设施领域强化执法的趋势，凸显云服务提供商面临的 IP 合规风险。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国云企业及出口商需警惕 SEP 权利人利用专项实体在欧洲发起批量诉讼。建议提前开展 FTO 分析，评估专利池许可策略，并关注 UPC 诉讼动态以规避高额赔偿风险。

news_items.title: Research: Female participation in inventorship
news_items.title_zh: 研究：女性参与发明人身份的分析
news_items.summary: Analysis of female representation among inventors listed on UK published patent applications from 1997 to 2024.
news_items.content: Analysis of female representation among inventors listed on UK published patent applications from 1997 to 2024.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布研究报告，分析了 1997 年至 2024 年间英国已公布专利申请中列明的女性发明人代表比例。该数据旨在揭示长期趋势下女性在技术创新领域的参与度变化，为制定促进多样性与包容性的知识产权政策提供实证依据，反映英国在专利生态系统中对性别平等的关注。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧洲市场对创新多样性的重视趋势，优化研发团队结构以提升 ESG 评级。在英申请专利时，准确登记所有发明人信息，避免因合规细节影响企业形象或政府资助资格。

news_items.title: 1 April 2026: Update to the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) fees
news_items.title_zh: 2026 年 4 月 1 日：专利合作条约（PCT）费用更新
news_items.summary: The Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) fee structure will change on 1 April 2026
news_items.content: The Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) fee structure will change on 1 April 2026
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布通知，确认专利合作条约（PCT）的费用结构将于 2026 年 4 月 1 日正式调整。作为 WIPO 管理下的关键国际专利申请体系，PCT 费用变动将直接波及所有经由英国或欧洲其他受理局提交国际申请的主体。此次更新旨在反映行政成本变化，申请人需提前规划预算，以避免因费用标准更迭导致的程序延误或额外支出，确保国际阶段顺利进入国家阶段。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利布局多依赖 PCT 途径，费用上调将增加海外维权与确权成本。建议企业重新核算 2026 年后的 IP 预算，对拟在该时间节点前后提交的申请进行策略性时间规划，并密切关注具体费率细则以优化资金配置。

news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需重新评估 2026 年后 PCT 国际阶段及进入国家阶段的预算成本", "建议根据新费率时间表优化申请提交节点，规避不必要的支出"]

news_items.title: FCJ: Requirements for Licensee Willingness under FRAND
news_items.title_zh: 德联邦法院：FRAND 抗辩中被许可人意愿的认定要求
news_items.summary: The German Federal Court of Justice clarifies the requirements for willingness to license in FRAND defence cases.
news_items.content:
Recent case law from the Munich patent courts has further refined the requirements for raising a competition law-based FRAND defence against injunctive relief based on standard-essential patents. Several decisions of the Regional Court of Munich I in 2025 and 2026 address in particular the assessment of an implementer’s willingness to take a license, the role of partial payments and security, and the determination of a permissible FRAND licensing range on the basis of comparable license agreements.

In its judgment of 8 January 2026 (Regional Court of Munich I, case no. 7 O 5007/25), the court emphasized that the assessment of willingness to license depends not only on formal declarations but also on the implementer’s concrete conduct in negotiations. In particular, the court considered it highly relevant whether the implementer pays at least an undisputed portion of the licence fee or provides adequate security, as such conduct may demonstrate that the implementer is not engaging in hold-out behaviour.

In another judgment of 5 February 2026 (Regional Court of Munich I, case no. 7 O 7655/25), the same chamber further elaborated on its approach to determining a FRAND royalty corridor based on comparable licence agreements. According to the court, comparable agreements may serve as a benchmark for identifying a permissible range of licence rates within which an offer will generally be considered FRAND-compliant.

Similarly, in its decision of 22 January 2026 (Regional Court of Munich I, case no. 7 O 4102/25) concerning patents allegedly essential to the H.265/HEVC video coding standard, the court rejected a competition law compulsory licence defence and held that the defendant had not demonstrated conduct sufficient to establish a successful FRAND defence.

Earlier, the Regional Court of Munich I had also addressed the international dimension of SEP disputes. In its judgment of 26 November 2025 (Regional Court of Munich I, case no. 21 O 12112/25), the court held that the competition law assessment of FRAND obligations with respect to German patents must, in principle, be carried out by German courts. It therefore considered attempts to obtain a court-ordered “interim licence” from a foreign court affecting German patents to be incompatible with the territorial nature of patent rights.

Taken together, these decisions illustrate how German first-instance courts are increasingly translating the framework established by the Court of Justice of the European Union in Huawei v ZTE into concrete procedural and economic criteria. Against this background, the recent judgment of the German Federal Court of Justice is of particular significance.

On 27 January 2026 (KZR 10/25), the Bundesgerichtshof dismissed HMD Global Oy’s appeal in the dispute with VoiceAge EVS GmbH. The judgment confirms the decisions of the Munich lower courts and further refines the standards governing FRAND defences against injunctive relief based on standard-essential patents.

The legal framework remains Article 102 TFEU and the principles developed by the Court of Justice of the European Union in Huawei v ZTE.

VoiceAge asserted claims based on a European patent declared essential to the EVS telecommunications standard. HMD argued that enforcement of the injunction would constitute an abuse of dominance, as it had been willing to take a licence on FRAND terms.

Both the Regional Court of Munich I and the Higher Regional Court of Munich rejected this defence, finding that HMD had not demonstrated sufficiently consistent willingness. The appeal raised, in particular, the question whether the Huawei framework must be applied in a strict chronological sequence and how security is to be assessed.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦法院在 HMD Global 诉 VoiceAge 案中驳回上诉，确认慕尼黑下级法院判决。裁判细化了标准必要专利（SEP）禁令救济中 FRAND 抗辩的适用标准，强调被许可人意愿不仅看声明，更需考察具体谈判行为、部分付款或担保提供情况，并明确德国法院对涉及德国专利的 FRAND 义务拥有管辖权。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 SEP 诉讼时，仅口头表达意愿不足以免除禁令风险，必须实质推进谈判并提供部分付款或担保。同时应避免寻求外国法院干预德国专利许可，以免被认定为非善意被许可人而败诉。

news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["实质履行谈判义务并提供担保是避免禁令的关键举措", "切勿试图通过外国法院裁决规避德国专利的地域管辖"]

news_items.title: Base station patent deals and disputes draw attention
news_items.title_zh: 基站专利交易与纠纷引发关注
news_items.summary: Recent Xiaomi and Oppo transactions invite curiosity while comments on licensing rates trigger patentee pushback
news_items.content: Recent Xiaomi and Oppo transactions invite curiosity while comments on licensing rates trigger patentee pushback
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 近期小米与 OPPO 的专利交易引发市场好奇，同时关于基站专利许可费率的评论激起专利权人强烈反弹。文章聚焦欧洲背景下标准必要专利（SEP）的商业动态，指出许可费率分歧正成为争议焦点，反映了当前欧洲专利许可市场的紧张局势及各方博弈。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严苛的 SEP 许可审查与费率挑战。建议密切跟踪欧洲判例，建立合规费率评估模型，避免激进言论引发诉讼，并通过多元化谈判策略降低在欧运营的法律与财务风险。

news_items.title: AI-assisted minute-taking extended to Unitary Patent oral proceedings
news_items.title_zh: EPO 将 AI 辅助笔录扩展至统一专利口头程序
news_items.summary: EPO extends AI‑assisted minute‑taking to Unitary Patent proceedings, improving efficiency, accuracy and consistency while ensuring full human oversight and data protection
news_items.content: Following a successful pilot phase, the EPO is extending the use of AI to support minute-taking in oral proceedings before the Unitary Patent Protection Division (UPPD). Following the positive results of the 2025 pilot, the European Patent Office (EPO) is extending AI-assisted minute-taking to oral proceedings before the Unitary Patent Protection Division. The approach is already used in oral proceedings in examination, opposition, before the Receiving Section and the Legal Division. This step further integrates AI-supported tools into the patent-granting process, while maintaining full human oversight and responsibility for the final minutes. How AI supports the work of divisions Audio is recorded via the videoconferencing system, and a transcript is generated using an AI tool. Shortly after the oral proceedings, the transcript is processed by the division with the support of another AI tool to produce draft minutes. After review, the minutes are finalised by the division in accordance with Rule 124(3) EPC. In line with data protection and procedural safeguards, the recording and its transcription are deleted once the minutes are issued to the parties. Lessons from the 2025 pilot The pilot helped refine and accelerate transcript generation. Examination and opposition divisions reported improvements in the consistency, structure and language of minutes, which were also welcomed by parties to the proceedings. Further information
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 继 2025 年试点成功后，欧洲专利局（EPO）正式将 AI 辅助笔录技术应用范围扩大至统一专利保护部门（UPPD）的口头程序。该流程利用 AI 生成录音转录稿并起草会议纪要，全程保留人工最终审核权以确保准确性。此举旨在提升审查、异议及统一专利程序的效率与一致性，同时严格遵守数据保护规定，录音在纪要发布后即被删除。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧参与统一专利诉讼或异议时，需适应 AI 辅助的高效程序节奏。建议提前准备高质量口头陈述，利用程序一致性优势，同时关注数据隐私合规，确保在人工复核环节有效传达核心技术与法律观点。

news_items.title: EPO and IP Australia PCT pilot programme enters into force starting 1 March 2026
news_items.title_zh: EPO 与澳知识产权局 PCT 试点项目于 2026 年启动
news_items.summary: Under a two-year pilot programme between the EPO and IP Australia that started 1 March 2026, Australian applicants can designate the EPO as their International Searching Authority (ISA) under the PCT.
news_items.content: As of 1 March 2026, Australian applicants are able to designate the EPO as their International Searching Authority (ISA) and International Preliminary Examining Authority (IPEA) under the Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT), marking the start of a two-year pilot programme. ​The programme is open to applicants filing their international applications either with IP Australia or with the International Bureau of the World Intellectual Property Organization as Receiving Office. Benefits for applicants By choosing the EPO as their ISA, Australian applicants can benefit from an accelerated route to high-quality European patent protection. With an international search report from the EPO, these applicants can bypass the supplementary search when entering the European regional phase, saving time and cost. They will also receive a 75% reduction in European regional phase examination fees if they requested international preliminary examination under PCT Chapter II with the EPO. The EPO’s international search report provides a solid basis for taking informed decisions on whether to enter the European phase and national phases before other offices. Once granted, a European patent can be validated in all 39 EPO member states and in seven additional extension or validation states. As an alternative or in addition to validating the European patent in individual countries, the patent proprietor can request unitary effect, resulting in a Unitary Patent that provides uniform patent protection in 18 EU Member States. Further information
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2026 年 3 月 1 日起，EPO 与澳大利亚知识产权局开启为期两年的 PCT 试点。澳洲申请人可指定 EPO 为国际检索和初步审查单位。此举允许申请人进入欧洲区域阶段时免除补充检索，并在请求第二章审查后享受 75% 官费减免，旨在加速高质量欧洲专利保护获取。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 虽试点针对澳洲申请人，但彰显 EPO 优化 PCT 流程趋势。中企应关注此类双边合作是否扩展至中国，提前布局利用 EPO 作为 ISA 以优化欧洲专利组合成本与效率。

news_items.title: EPO’s next-generation search tool for examiners now used in over 40 national patent offices worldwide
news_items.title_zh: EPO 新一代审查检索工具已在全球四十余国局启用
news_items.summary: A new search era has formally commenced: the next-generation tool ANSERA-based SEARCH was developed by the European Patent Office (EPO) in collaboration with national patent offices across the EPO member states.
news_items.content: A new search era has formally commenced: the next-generation tool ANSERA-based SEARCH was developed by the European Patent Office (EPO) in collaboration with national patent offices across the EPO member states. The tool now provides more than 2 500 examiners at national patent offices around the world with a futureproof search solution for handling increasingly complex prior art, enabling its predecessor, EPOQUE Net, to be decommissioned. The successful introduction of ANSERA-based SEARCH at more than 40 national patent offices marks a major milestone in the EPO’s commitment to delivering excellence through the use of digital tools, as set out in the Strategic Plan 2028. The search solution is closely based on the EPO's own highly sophisticated ANSERA tool. Upholding the highest standards of patent quality internationally ANSERA-based SEARCH (AbS) is a highly advanced solution that enables examiners at national offices to perform fast, efficient and in-depth searches across an exceptional range of databases covering worldwide patent documents and a unique, extensive collection of non-patent literature. Compared to its predecessor, AbS supports more sophisticated workflows: it manages enormous document volumes, intelligently ranks results, provides semantic-like retrieval features, and allows examiners to organise prior art in a clear and structured manner – all critical for accurately assessing novelty and inventive step. Strengthening the innovation ecosystem AbS will continue to contribute to harmonising high-quality search practices, strengthening co-operation with patent offices across the globe, just as EPOQUE Net did since it was developed by the EPO in the late 1980s. Recent developments confirm the European Patent Office’s leadership in patent quality and its commitment to providing advanced tools, shared data and common standards that support the world’s innovation ecosystem. Further information
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）开发的基于 ANSERA 的新一代检索工具 SEARCH，已在全球超过 40 个国家专利局投入使用。该工具取代了旧的 EPOQUE Net 系统，为 2500 多名审查员提供处理复杂现有技术的未来型解决方案。其具备智能排序、语义检索及海量数据管理能力，旨在统一全球高质量检索实践，巩固 EPO 在专利质量领域的领导地位，支持全球创新生态系统。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请将面临更严苛且高效的现有技术检索。建议提前进行深度 FTO 分析，利用同类先进工具自查，确保新颖性与创造性论证充分，以应对审查标准的技术性升级。

news_items.title: President’s mission to Hungary
news_items.title_zh: EPO 局长访问匈牙利共商欧洲专利网络未来
news_items.summary: President Campinos attended the 130th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO) and met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas to discuss the future of the European Patent Network.
news_items.content: On 3 March, EPO President António Campinos took part in the celebrations marking the 130 th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO). During his visit he met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas to discuss intellectual property priorities at both the national and European level. The mission also formed part of the Office’s ongoing consultations with member states as it prepares a new Scenarios for the Future study, which will explore the potential evolution of the innovation ecosystem, European patent system and European Patent Network (EPN) over the next two decades. Reinforced cooperation, harmonised practices and shared digital tools HIPO commemorated its 130 th anniversary with an intellectual property conference on 3 March, which President Campinos opened with a keynote speech. In his remarks, he addressed the future of the European patent system and highlighted the importance of maintaining high quality and legal certainty in the face of rapid technological change and increasing global competition. He also underlined the importance of further strengthening the European Patent Network, including deeper integration through the Unitary Patent system, to reinforce Europe as a coherent technology market. Emphasising close co-operation between the EPO and national IP offices, he stressed the value of harmonised practices and shared digital tools. In the President’s meeting with Mr Farkas following the conference, the discussion covered similar ground, exploring how the European patent system can remain resilient, competitive and responsive in the face of technological, economic and geopolitical developments. Particular attention was given to the modernisation of the European Patent Network, including the enhancement of digital co-operation, convergence of practices, capacity-building and improved joint support for startups and SMEs. Further information
news_items.ai_summary_zh: EPO 局长坎皮诺斯出席匈牙利知识产权局成立 130 周年活动，并与该局主席法卡斯会晤。双方聚焦欧洲专利系统韧性，探讨通过统一专利制度深化网络整合、协调审查实践及共享数字工具，以应对技术变革并支持中小企业创新。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧洲专利网络数字化整合趋势，利用协调化程序降低多国申请成本。建议密切跟踪统一专利制度在匈牙利的落地进展，优化在欧洲新兴市场的专利布局策略以增强竞争力。

news_items.title: Avanci enters market for licensing wi-fi SEPs
news_items.title_zh: Avanci 进军 Wi-Fi 标准必要专利许可市场
news_items.summary: Avanci has expanded beyond cellular connectivity into wi-fi technology and launched a new platform for wi-fi 6 and wi-fi 7 SEPs.
news_items.content:
Firms & Experts Sponsored: Firm Profiles Thum&Partner Maikowski & Ninnemann BARDEHLE PAGENBERG View all Firm Profiles Sponsored: Expert Profiles Heinrich Georg Pannen Alexander Ortlieb Ingo Bauer, Ph.D. (WHUT) View all Expert Profiles Sponsored Content UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP View all Sponsored Content

UPC Provisional Measures: Dual Validi… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Lessons from dapagliflozin in the UK:… by Pinsent Masons LLP

The Unified Patent Court and Damages:… by Simmons + Simmons LLP

Patent pool Avanci has now expanded beyond cellular connectivity into wi-fi technology. It has launched a new platform covering wi-fi 6 and, in future, wi-fi 7 standard essential patents. German luxury carmaker Mercedes-Benz has signed on as the first licensee.

Avanci now joins the likes of patent pool Sisvel in licensing wi-fi technology. Today, Avanci announced the launch of its new wi-fi licensing platform aimed specifically at the automotive industry. Its first programme under the new platform, Avanci Wi-Fi 6 Vehicle, consolidates essential wi-fi 6 patent rights into a single licence for automotive manufacturers.

The new platform will also cover SEPs for wi-fi 7.

The Avanci Wi-Fi 6 Vehicle programme brings together ten patent owners. These are Atlas Global, BlackBerry, Canon, ISG, KPN, Malike Innovations, Meizu, NEC, NTT, and Oppo. Avanci states that the licence covers thousands of patented technologies essential to implementing wi-fi 6 connectivity in vehicles.

Avanci has not publicly disclosed the royalty rate per-vehicle, but says the figure is available to prospective licensees on a confidential basis. An early licensee discount applies for companies that conclude a licence agreement before their first sale of a wi-fi-6-compliant vehicle, or within six months of the programme’s launch.

Mercedes-Benz is the first car manufacturer to have signed a patent sublicence agreement under the programme. Formerly known as Daimler, the carmaker and Avanci pool member Nokia were previously involved in a fierce dispute in German courts over mobile communication SEPs in the so-called connected car wars.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 专利池运营商 Avanci 宣布将其业务从蜂窝通信扩展至 Wi-Fi 技术领域，推出涵盖 Wi-Fi 6 及未来 Wi-Fi 7 标准必要专利的新许可平台，首期聚焦汽车行业。梅赛德斯 - 奔驰成为首位被许可人。该平台整合了包括诺基亚、OPPO 等十家权利人的专利，旨在为车企提供一站式许可方案，费率保密但设有早期签约折扣。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出口欧洲将面临新的 Wi-Fi 专利许可成本与合规要求。建议相关企业评估 Avanci 平台费率合理性，关注 FRAND 原则履行情况，并提前布局谈判策略以规避潜在诉讼风险。

news_items.title: Requests to amend a patent after grant
news_items.title_zh: 专利授权后的修改申请指南
news_items.summary: Applications received to amend the specification of a patent under sections 27 and 75 before the court and the Office.
news_items.content: Applications received to amend the specification of a patent under sections 27 and 75 before the court and the Office.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布关于专利授权后修改说明书的官方指南。内容涵盖依据《1977 年专利法》第 27 条向局方提出修改，以及第 75 条在法院诉讼期间提出修改的申请流程。该文件明确了不同路径下的受理标准与审查实践，旨在指导权利人及代理人规范操作，确保修改请求符合法定形式与实质要求，以维持专利有效性或应对无效挑战。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英持有专利时，若遇无效风险或需澄清权利要求，应善用此机制。建议提前评估修改策略，避免因程序瑕疵导致权利丧失，同时注意法院与局方程序差异，选择最优维权或确权路径。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["明确区分向 UKIPO 提出申请与在法院诉讼中申请修改的不同法律依据", "详述专利授权后修改说明书的具体流程、所需文件及审查标准", "强调修改不得扩大保护范围，需符合新颖性与创造性等实质要件"]

news_items.title: Avanci unveils Wi-Fi 6 Vehicle programme with Mercedes as first licensee
news_items.title_zh: Avanci 推出 Wi-Fi 6 车载计划，奔驰成首家被许可人
news_items.summary: New platform has “significantly lower” rates than cellular SEP offering, says programme lead Qudus Olaniran
news_items.content: New platform has “significantly lower” rates than cellular SEP offering, says programme lead Qudus Olaniran
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 专利池运营商 Avanci 正式推出面向汽车行业的 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利许可计划，德国车企梅赛德斯 - 奔驰成为首个签约被许可人。项目负责人强调，该新平台的许可费率显著低于其现有的蜂窝通信 SEP 许可方案，旨在为车联网领域提供更灵活且成本可控的知识产权解决方案。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及零部件出口商应关注 Wi-Fi 6 许可新路径，评估纳入该计划对降低合规成本的价值。建议提前梳理自身专利组合，对比 Avanci 新费率与单独谈判差异，优化在欧洲市场的知识产权战略布局。

news_items.title: BYD sued at UPC over former LG Energy Solution battery patents
news_items.title_zh: 比亚迪在统一专利法院遭前 LG 电池专利诉讼
news_items.summary: BMS Innovations lawsuit marks first time BYD has been sued at UPC post-Avanci licence
news_items.content: BMS Innovations lawsuit marks first time BYD has been sued at UPC post-Avanci licence
news_items.ai_summary_zh: BMS Innovations 在统一专利法院起诉比亚迪，指控其侵犯原属 LG 能源解决方案的电池专利。此案标志比亚迪在获得 Avanci 许可后，首次成为统一专利法院被告，凸显即使持有部分许可，特定专利组合仍可能引发独立诉讼风险。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕“许可覆盖盲区”，Avanci 等池化许可未必涵盖所有必要专利。建议出口欧洲前开展针对性 FTO 分析，特别关注非 SEP 核心部件专利，并评估统一专利法院集中管辖带来的泛欧禁令风险。

news_items.title: Copyright and Champagne labels: French court confirms rejection of the accessory theory and tacit assignment
news_items.title_zh: 法国法院驳回附属理论，确认香槟标签版权侵权
news_items.summary: France's Douai Court of Appeal has confirmed that a work reproduced on Champagne bottles without authorisation constitutes copyright infringement.
news_items.content:
The decision reiterates that neither the accessory theory nor the tacit assignment protects companies exploiting a copyright-protected work without a clear contract, as Florence Chapin reports.

In its 20 November 2025 ruling , France’s Douai Court of Appeal reinforced the strict interpretation of the author's exclusive right to exploit their work. Rejecting both the accessory theory and the existence of a tacit assignment of economic rights, it upheld the conviction of a Champagne producer for copyright infringement due to the unauthorised reproduction of a work on its labels, packaging and digital media.

In 2015, an artist was commissioned to create a work entitled "The Oak" by a Champagne house, which had purchased the physical sculpture for €4,000. The Champagne house subsequently reproduced this work on its bottle labels, packaging and website.

The artist then filed a lawsuit in the Lille Judicial Court seeking recognition of copyright infringement and an injunction to stop the unauthorised use of the artwork. The court ruled in favour of the artist, a decision against which the Champagne house appealed.

The accessory theory, based on French case law, allows, in certain circumstances, for the classification of an act as copyright infringement to be dismissed when the protected work appears in a secondary and incidental manner within a larger work.

It has been accepted to occur when the work:

is not the primary object of the reproduction;

and is not sought for its own sake.

Legal scholars emphasise, however, that this theory does not constitute an independent exception to copyright law, but rather a method for assessing the substantial nature of the reproduction. It is based on a concrete analysis of the economic function of the work within the context of the dispute.

In this case, the work "The Oak" was reproduced:
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 法国杜埃上诉法院维持原判，认定香槟生产商未经授权在标签及数字媒体复制艺术作品构成侵权。法院明确拒绝适用“附属理论”及“默示转让”抗辩，强调即便作品物理所有权转移，若无明确合同，经济权利仍归作者所有。此案重申了作者专有权利的严格解释原则。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧采购艺术品或设计时，须签署书面版权许可协议，切勿误以为购买实物即获使用权。面对侵权诉讼，依赖“附属使用”或“默示授权”等法式抗辩风险极高，应提前完成权利链条清理。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院驳回附属理论，认定涉案作品非次要或偶然出现", "明确物理作品买卖不等于版权经济权利的默示转让", "重申无明确合同时，作者保留对其作品的独家 exploitation 权"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采购实体艺术品务必同步签署书面版权授权合同", "在法涉诉时避免盲目援引附属理论作为免责抗辩"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 法国上诉法院确认版权侵权判决，明确附属理论不适用，对欧企合规具重要参考价值。

news_items.title: BREAKING: Imminent new referral to the EBA on the relevance of G1/24 to added matter
news_items.title_zh: 突发：T 873/24 案就 G1/24 适用于新增事项向 EBA 转呈问题
news_items.summary: The Board of Appeal in T 873/24 has decided to refer questions to the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) on the application of G1/24 (claim interpretation) to the assessment of added matter. According to the minutes of oral proceedings, the Board of Appeal concluded that the case hinged on whether the order from G1/24 that the description and drawings should always be consulted for claim
news_items.content: The Board of Appeal in T 873/24 has decided to refer questions to the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) on the application of G1/24 (claim interpretation) to the assessment of added matter. According to the minutes of oral proceedings, the Board of Appeal concluded that the case hinged on whether the order from G1/24 that the description and drawings should always be consulted for claim
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局技术上诉委员会在 T 873/24 案中决定，就 G1/24 号决定（关于权利要求解释）是否适用于“新增事项”评估这一问题，向扩大上诉委员会（EBA）转呈。案件核心争议在于，G1/24 确立的“必须结合说明书和附图解释权利要求”这一原则，在判断修改是否超出原申请范围时是否具有同等约束力。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请需关注解释规则变化，避免因依赖单一文本导致修改被拒。建议重新审视撰写策略，确保说明书充分支持各类潜在修改，以应对更严格的审查标准。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉委员会认定案件取决于 G1/24 解释原则的适用范围", "正式向扩大上诉委员会转呈关于新增事项审查的法律问题", "明确说明书与附图在权利要求解释及修改审查中的关联性"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需强化说明书对权利要求修改的支撑力度以防新增事项驳回", "密切关注 EBA 最终裁决以调整欧洲专利申请文件撰写策略"]

news_items.title: Regeneron settles several aflibercept disputes following European injunctions
news_items.title_zh: 再生元在欧洲获禁令后达成多项阿柏西普纠纷和解
news_items.summary: Biologic innovator had secured a wave of agreements in wake of recent injunction victories
news_items.content: Biologic innovator had secured a wave of agreements in wake of recent injunction victories
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 生物制药 innovator 再生元（Regeneron）在近期于欧洲获得针对阿柏西普（aflibercept）产品的禁令胜利后，迅速与多家竞争对手达成一系列和解协议。该动态表明统一专利法院（UPC）签发的禁令具有强大的市场威慑力，迫使被告方回到谈判桌，从而快速解决跨国专利争端，确立了权利人在欧洲市场的排他性优势。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出海需高度警惕 UPC 禁令的即时杀伤力，其可迫使企业迅速和解。建议提前开展 FTO 分析，评估欧洲专利风险，并制定包含诉讼与商业谈判的综合应对预案，避免陷入被动。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["再生元凭借欧洲禁令胜利，迫使多方达成阿柏西普专利和解", "UPC 禁令展现出高效威慑力，加速了跨国专利纠纷的商业解决", "生物药企利用欧洲统一司法机制，成功巩固核心产品市场独占权"]

news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文深入分析英国最高法院在 Emotional Perception 案中的判决，该判决确立了英国判断计算机实施发明的新测试标准。文章探讨此新标准如何使英国专利实践进一步向欧洲专利局（EPO）的审查惯例靠拢，并评估两者间剩余的具体差异，对人工智能领域专利布局具有关键指导意义。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英申请 AI 专利时，应参照欧专局标准优化权利要求撰写，利用英欧标准趋同降低跨国布局成本。同时需密切关注新测试法的具体适用细节，避免因审查标准微调导致授权风险，及时调整欧洲整体专利策略。

news_items.title: Injunction need not list products, but 40 auxiliary requests trigger tabular clarity requirements
news_items.title_zh: UPC 裁定禁令无需列明产品但大量辅助请求需表格化
news_items.summary: GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals v Moderna – The Hague Local Division, 18 February 2026, UPC_CFI_616/2025 and UPC_CFI_1439/2025
news_items.content: Injunction need not list products, but 40 auxiliary requests trigger tabular clarity requirements GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals v Moderna – The Hague Local Division, 18 February 2026, UPC_CFI_616/2025 and UPC_CFI_1439/2025 (procedural order, Rules 263 and 9 RoP; Rule 30.1 RoP) Introduction In a procedural order, the judge-rapporteur of the Hague Local Division dealt with three procedural points in a pharma dispute: (i) GSK’s attempt under Rule 263 RoP to add Moderna’s updated vaccine product mNEXSPIKE to the definition of the alleged infringing products, (ii) Moderna’s Rule 9 RoP application seeking to strike allegedly late-filed evidence and claim-construction/infringement argumentation, and (iii) the reasonableness of GSK’s 40 conditional auxiliary requests filed with its Rule 30.1 RoP amendment application. Amendment of the statement of claim under Rule 263 RoP GSK sought leave to amend its infringement case to expressly include mNEXSPIKE, pointing to the EMA’s positive opinion of 11 December 2025 and arguing that mNEXSPIKE was essentially an upgrade with the same relevant lipid composition as the previously identified products. Moderna opposed the amendment, relying inter alia on earlier public information in the US. The Court granted the Rule 263 application. It stressed, first, that there is generally no need to limit an injunction request to specific product names: following the Court of Appeal’s guidance (Abbott v Sibio), relief need not be tied to a closed list of “infringing products”. On the wording of GSK’s statement of claim, the requested injunction already covered “further versions or variants”. Accordingly, the express addition of mNEXSPIKE was not strictly necessary. However, even if treated as an amendment of the case, the requirements of Rule 263.2 RoP were met. The Court accepted that GSK could not, with reasonable diligence, have been expected to plead a Europe-relevant product launch before the EMA’s December 2025 step, and it found no unreasonable hindrance to Moderna’s defence given the asserted continuity with the already pleaded products. Rule 30.1 RoP: 40 auxiliary requests and the “speedy proceedings” constraint With its reply/defence to the counterclaim, GSK filed 40 conditional auxiliary requests. Moderna argued that the number was excessive, non-convergent and potentially even higher if combinations were allowed, and it sought a restriction to 10 and/or dismissal of requests en bloc. The Court was “not convinced” (at that stage) that 40 auxiliary requests (introducing at least 18 new features) were reasonable. However, rather than imposing a numerical cap or striking requests en bloc , the Court ordered GSK to file within one week a comprehensive table identifying, for each auxiliary request, the introduced feature combination and the invalidity attack(s) addressed. Any further combinations would require approval under Rule 30.2 RoP. Rule 9 RoP: late-filed evidence and argumentation Moderna sought (or alternatively requested the Court to disregard) various parts of GSK’s reply, including Cryo-TEM images, related expert analysis, and extensive claim-construction/infringement sections said to go beyond a proper reply. The Court dismissed the Rule 9 application. It held that the contested material was, in the circumstances, a permissible response to Moderna’s non-infringement and claim-construction positions and largely expanded on points already made in the statement of claim. The front-loaded nature of UPC proceedings does not require claimants to anticipate every defence at the outset. Excluding the material would, on the facts, interfere with GSK’s right to be heard, and Moderna still had sufficient opportunity to respond in a further written round and at the oral hearing. Moderna’s request to stay deadlines pending the Rule 9 decision was also dismissed. Takeaways A claimant does not need to frame its injunction request as a closed list of accused products; “variants” within the claim scope may already be covered, reducing the procedural need for Rule 263 amendments. The Court was sceptic as to whether 40 auxiliary requests are “reasonable” under Rule 30.1 RoP, but preferred case-management tools (mandatory tabular mapping to objections) over blunt caps or en-bloc dismissal at such early stage of the proceedings. Front-loading is not strictly required where the reply genuinely responds to pleaded defences; the right to be heard and the availability of a further written round can weigh against exclusion. GlaxoSmithKline Biologicals v Moderna – The Hague Local Division, 18 February 2026, UPC_CFI_616/2025 and UPC_CFI_1439/2025 (procedural order, Rules 263 and 9 RoP; Rule 30.1 RoP) In a procedural order, the judge-rapporteur of the Hague Local Division dealt with three procedural points in a pharma dispute: (i) GSK’s attempt under Rule 263 RoP to add Moderna’s updated vaccine product mNEXSPIKE to the definition of the alleged infringing products, (ii) Moderna’s Rule 9 RoP application seeking to strike allegedly late-filed evidence and claim-construction/infringement argumentation, and (iii) the reasonableness of GSK’s 40 conditional auxiliary requests filed with its Rule 30.1 RoP amendment application. news_items.title: Brexit "cloned" trademarks in 2026: the end of a transitional period that could affect your protection in the UK or the EU
news_items.title_zh: 2026 年脱欧克隆商标过渡期终结：英欧使用要求彻底分离
news_items.summary: From 1 January 2026, UK trademarks require UK use and EU marks require EU use. Learn how Brexit impacts cloned marks and revocation risks ⚖️
news_items.content:
When the UK left the European Union, thousands of businesses faced uncertainty over the status of their trademarks. EU trademarks would no longer extend to the UK, even though many brand owners traded in both territories. To prevent disruption, the UK government created “cloned” registrations: UK rights that mirror the corresponding EU marks in the UK trademark register. This practical arrangement came with a five-year transition period, which expired on 31 December 2025.

From 1 January 2026, an important change has occurred: use in the EU will no longer help you keep a “cloned” UK trademark on the UK register, and use in the UK will no longer help you maintain an EU trademark (EUTM). Trademark owners who haven’t actively used their marks in the right territory face real risks.

Here’s what you need to understand about this important deadline.

The UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) automatically generated these identical marks when Brexit took effect. Every EU trademark registered before 1 January 2021 received a UK counterpart.

These marks inherited everything from their EU parents: filing dates, priority dates, registration dates.

They became independent UK rights, subject to UK national law. But the rules on “genuine use” and reputation were temporarily adapted, allowing EU use to be taken into account during the post-Brexit transitional period.

Trademark rights can, in theory, last indefinitely. But the system relies on a simple rule: use the mark, or risk losing it. If you don’t genuinely use your mark for five continuous years, anyone can challenge it and potentially have it removed from the register.

The Brexit Withdrawal Agreement recognized this would create immediate problems. Imagine a French company selling exclusively in France and Germany. Before Brexit, the EU mark covered the UK even without sales there. After Brexit, their new UK mark would instantly be vulnerable to cancellation for non-use.

The solution was a transitional period. For five years, businesses could rely on use anywhere in the EU (including pre-Brexit UK use) to defend their “cloned” UK marks. Similarly, UK businesses could count UK use toward their EU marks. This mutual recognition helped preserve the status quo and provided continuity in both systems throughout the transition.

From 1 January 2026, the territories split completely. UK trademarks require UK use. EU trademarks require EU use.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国脱欧后生成的克隆商标享有五年过渡期，允许利用欧盟使用证据维持英国商标。该过渡期于 2025 年底届满。自 2026 年 1 月 1 日起，英国克隆商标必须依赖英国境内的真实使用来维持效力，欧盟使用不再被认可；反之亦然。未在规定领土内积极使用的商标将面临因连续五年未使用而被撤销的风险。

news_items.title: R.262A applications required to maintain confidentiality in UPC Proceedings | EIP
news_items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院：维持机密性须依规则 262A 正式申请
news_items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarified the necessity of formal applications to maintain confidentiality in Unified Patent Court (UPC) proceedings when disclosing ordered information. This ruling arose from a dispute involving patent infringement and confidentiality claims between EOFlow and Insulet.
news_items.content: An expert team of litigators, patent attorneys and consultants ready to help you protect and commercialise your business. An expert team of litigators, patent attorneys and commercial IP lawyers ready to help you protect and commercialise your business. UPC_CoA_930/2025, order of 29 January 2026 (1) The Court of Appeal held that a party must file an application pursuant to rule 262A of the Rules of Procedure (“RoP”) if it wishes to keep confidential information it has been ordered to disclose in proceedings. Absent such an application, there is no implied confidentiality of documents disclosed in UPC proceedings. The wider action between the parties concerns the infringement of Insulet’s patent covering fluid delivery devices for therapeutic liquids. In May 2025, the Court of Appeal of the UPC granted Insulet a preliminary injunction and, inter alia, ordered EOFlow to disclose i) information about the origin and distribution of the infringing devices in UPC states and ii) the identity of any third parties involved in the production or distribution of the affected devices in UPC states. (2) EOFlow claimed that these documents had been filed as “HC” (Highly Confidential) on the Case Management System but, crucially, EOFlow did not file a corresponding application under r. 262A RoP that information in these documents be restricted. The present order arises from an appeal of a December 2025 decision of the Milan Central Division imposing penalties on EOFlow for breaches of the interim injunction. Notably, in the first instance proceedings, Insulet had relied on business information that EOFlow had been ordered to disclose in earlier preliminary injunction proceedings. Insulet was not treating this business information as being confidential. EOFlow argued that its, and its distribution partner’s, information disclosed under the previous order was confidential and Insulet’s use of such information in subsequent proceedings should not be allowed. The Court of Appeal held that an application under r. 262A RoP is essential where a party wishes to maintain the confidentiality of information it has been ordered to disclose. No implicit obligations of confidentiality exist. As Insulet has missed the 14-day window in which a party must make an application to maintain confidentiality in their documents after they have disclosed them to the court, the information was no longer confidential and could be used freely by Insulet. (The court reserved its decision on EOFlow’s other requests regarding third party access to the information.) This case demonstrates that it is imperative for parties to make applications under r. 262A RoP where they wish to maintain confidentiality in information and documents they are ordered to disclose in UPC proceedings. Failure to do so will likely result in the confidentiality in those materials being lost, and may allow the opposing party to use information gathered in UPC proceedings elsewhere without limitation. https://www.unifiedpatentcourt.org/en/node/160311
news_items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉法院在 EOFlow 与 Insulet 专利纠纷中裁定，当事人若希望保持被命令披露信息的机密性，必须依据程序规则第 262A 条提交正式申请。法院强调不存在默示保密义务，因 EOFlow 未在披露后 14 天内提出申请，导致相关信息丧失机密性，对方可自由使用。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中需严格遵循程序时限，披露敏感信息时务必同步提交 262A 保密申请，避免因程序疏忽导致商业机密泄露并在后续诉讼中处于被动。

news_items.title: The UK Competition and Markets Authority announces its first ever fine under new consumer enforcement powers
news_items.title_zh: 英国 CMA 依据新消费者执法权开出首张罚单
news_items.summary: Key takeaways The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) announced on 13 February 2026 that it has fined Euro Car Parks nearly half a million pounds...
news_items.content: In July 2025, the CMA issued Euro Car Parks ( ECP ) with an information notice under the Consumer Rights Act 2015. Despite seven attempts to deliver by registered mail, by hand and by email to company directors, ECP did not engage with the CMA. ECP only provided a response after the CMA notified it of an intention to impose a fine. ECP had attempted to justify its lack of response: The CMA rejected these explanations and concluded that ECP had no “reasonable excuse” for failing to comply. It was incumbent upon ECP, as an organisation, to ensure that official correspondence is properly managed and that statutory obligations are met in a timely manner. The CMA noted that multiple attempts to contact ECP would be unusual for a scam and ECP took no action to verify with the CMA. In December 2025, the CMA issued a fine of £473,000. Under the DMCC, the CMA may impose penalties of up to 1% of a company’s annual worldwide turnover for non-compliance with information notices. The £473,000 penalty represents approximately 75% of the maximum fine available, reflecting the egregiousness of ECP’s actions. ECP sought an injunction in the High Court to stop the CMA from naming it. The Court refused the application in early February. ECP has also appealed the fine to the High Court. In November last year, the CMA opened its first consumer enforcement cases against eight major UK companies (see our commentary here ). In a recent speech , the Acting Executive Director of Consumer Protection announced that the CMA had issued 29 targeted information requests to businesses. The penalty against ECP, imposed before an investigation was even opened, highlights the CMA’s continued efforts under its enhanced consumer protection powers. It is also another example of the implementation of CMA’s “4Ps” agenda as it seeks to encourage “pace, predictability, proportionality and process” across the CMA’s portfolio of competition and consumer work.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争与市场管理局（CMA）对 Euro Car Parks 处以 47.3 万英镑罚款，因其无视信息通知且无正当理由。这是 CMA 依据《数字市场、竞争和消费者法》行使新权力的首例处罚，金额占上限 75%。涉事企业申请禁令失败并已提起上诉，凸显监管机构强化执法力度与程序合规要求。

news_items.title: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
news_items.title_zh: EUIPO 支持全欧打击假药重大行动
news_items.summary: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines
news_items.content: EUIPO supports major Europe-wide action against counterfeit medicines EUR 32 million worth of fake medicines and illicit doping substances have been seized across Europe in Operation SHIELD VI, a major operation led by Europol with the support of the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO), alongside other international partners. Carried out between April and November 2025, the operation brought together authorities from 30 European countries. It led to nearly 500 arrests, more than 3,300 prosecutions and investigations into over 40 organised crime groups. In addition, 66 websites used to sell counterfeit medicines were shut down. The seized products included over 3 million fake pills and nearly 300 000 illegal packages of doping substances, including Semaglutide-based products marketed as fat-burners. Some of these counterfeit medicines closely resemble legitimate products, including opioids, which significantly increases the risk of poisoning and overdose. Through its support to Operation SHIELD VI, the EUIPO contributed its expertise in tackling intellectual property crime and analysing online infringement trends linked to counterfeit pharmaceuticals, reinforcing coordinated action to protect consumers and legitimate businesses across the EU. A growing threat for European consumers According to the latest data, trade in counterfeit and illicit medicines is on the rise, coming at a high cost not only for individual consumers but also for society as a whole. Beyond infringing intellectual property rights, these products pose a serious threat to public health, as they often contain dangerous, substandard or unknown substances. The misuse of prescription and over-the-counter medicines, together with the growth of unregulated websites selling fake medicines, continues to fuel illegal markets and further complicates enforcement efforts by making detection and interception more difficult. To better protect consumers from counterfeit medicines and promote safer online purchases, the EUIPO, together with the European Medicines Agency (EMA) and Europol, launched the campaign “Stay on top – EU vs Fake Medicines”. The initiative raises awareness about the risks of buying medicines from unverified online sources and provides guidance on how to identify authorised sellers.

news_items.title: Long arm not available for amended patent | EIP
news_items.title_zh: UPC 裁定：修改后的统一专利无法延伸至瑞士禁令
news_items.summary: IMC Créations is a French company specialising in anti-theft systems for vehicles, particularly commercial vehicles. Among other things, it sells locks for the side and rear doors of commercial vehicles. Mul-T-Lock belongs to the Assa Abloy group and specialises in high security locking and access control systems, in particular pick-resistant keys and locks. IMC alleged that Mul-T-Lock’s MPV 1000 padlock infringes its unitary patent EP4153830 and the corresponding Swiss national validation.
news_items.content: An expert team of litigators, patent attorneys and consultants ready to help you protect and commercialise your business. An expert team of litigators, patent attorneys and commercial IP lawyers ready to help you protect and commercialise your business. UPC_CFI_702/2024 (Paris LD), decision of 16 January 2026 (1) IMC Créations is a French company specialising in anti-theft systems for vehicles, particularly commercial vehicles. Among other things, it sells locks for the side and rear doors of commercial vehicles. Mul-T-Lock belongs to the Assa Abloy group and specialises in high security locking and access control systems, in particular pick-resistant keys and locks. IMC alleged that Mul-T-Lock’s MPV 1000 padlock infringes its unitary patent EP4153830 and the corresponding Swiss national validation. During the proceedings, IMC unconditionally amended the patent, limiting it for the purposes of the unitary effect. This amendment played a decisive role in the court’s analysis of whether IMC could rely on the amended patent to seek an injunction in Switzerland, a nonUPC country. IMC explicitly sought a permanent injunction covering both France (UPC territory) and Switzerland, and argued that the UPC had jurisdiction to adjudicate infringement of the Swiss national part of the patent. It relied on the similarity of provisions between the Lugano Convention and Brussels I Regulation and pointed to other UPC decisions suggesting the possibility of effects beyond UPC Member States. MulTLock contested the UPC’s ability to rule on Swiss infringement, arguing first that infringement under Swiss national law had not been properly pleaded, and second that the UPC cannot rule on the validity of a national patent right outside UPC territory. According to the defendant, an injunction cannot be granted where the validity of the national part is in doubt, especially because IMC’s amendment implicitly acknowledged invalidity of the patent as originally granted. The cou rt held that it does not have jurisdiction to rule on the validity of a national part of a patent granted by a non UPC state, citing the CJEU decision BSHElectrolux (C339/22). While the UPC can hear infringement actions relating to national parts of patents from states bound by the Lugano Convention, this is subject to a critical limitation: the UPC must refuse or stay its assessment where there is a serious risk that the national part will be revoked by the court of the state of grant. Here, the court found that IMC’s unconditional amendment of the unitary patent created a “significant reasonable risk of invalidity” of the national (Swiss) part in its original granted form. By limiting the patent within the UPC proceedings to overcome inventive step objections, IMC implicitly acknowledged deficiencies in the granted version. Because the Swiss part remained in its granted form (the amendment applied only to the unitary patent), the court concluded that there was a serious risk that the Swiss courts could revoke the unamended Swiss designation. Consequently, the UPC declined to assess infringement in Switzerland and dismissed all claims based on the Swiss national part.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 法国 IMC 公司起诉 Mul-T-Lock 侵犯其统一专利及瑞士部分。IMC 在诉讼中无条件修改了统一专利权利要求。UPC 巴黎地方法院裁定，因修改暗示原授权版本存在无效风险，而瑞士部分未同步修改，故法院拒绝就瑞士部分颁发禁令，确立了 UPC 对非成员国专利行使长臂管辖的限制。
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧诉讼若涉及统一专利与非 UPC 国家（如瑞士、英国）组合，需谨慎对待权利要求修改。单方面为适应 UPC 标准而缩小保护范围，可能导致失去在非 UPC 国家的禁令救济，需统筹全局策略。
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 无权裁决非成员国授予的专利部分有效性问题", "单方修改统一专利暗示原版本缺陷，导致瑞士部分面临无效风险", "当存在严重撤销风险时，UPC 拒绝对非成员国部分行使管辖权"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["修改统一专利可能削弱对应国家部分的稳定性与执行力", "跨境维权需评估 UPC 裁决对非成员国专利效力的连带影响"]
news_items.ai_relevance_reason_zh: 涉及 UPC 对非成员国专利管辖权及修改后专利效力，核心议题。
